# omada_firewall_setting (Resource)

Manages the site-global Omada firewall settings (coarse blob): protocol conntrack timeouts and a handful of hardening toggles. This is a singleton per site: the object always exists, so import it (`<site_id>`) to adopt the live settings before managing. Create/Update overwrite the whole object via Modify; Delete is a no-op (the singleton is never removed, and Reset is never called). Targets the Open API v1 firewall surface (controller firmware such as 5.15.x).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Site ID that owns the settings. Provide it via a variable or data source so the
# value is not hard-coded in checked-in configurations.
variable "site_id" {
  type = string
}

# The firewall settings are a singleton per site: import the live object first
# (`tofu import omada_firewall_setting.default <site_id>`) so the baseline plan
# is a no-op before managing it. The whole object is overwritten on apply.
resource "omada_firewall_setting" "default" {
  site_id = var.site_id

  # Hardening toggles (security-relevant; set deliberately).
  broadcast_ping    = false # drop broadcast ping
  receive_redirects = false # ignore ICMP redirects
  send_redirects    = false # do not send ICMP redirects
  syn_cookies       = true  # SYN-flood cookie protection

  # Conntrack/protocol timeouts (seconds, 1-2097151).
  icmp            = 30
  other           = 600
  tcp_close       = 10
  tcp_close_wait  = 60
  tcp_established = 7440
  tcp_fin_wait    = 120
  tcp_last_ack    = 30
  tcp_syn_receive = 60
  tcp_syn_sent    = 120
  tcp_time_wait   = 120
  udp_other       = 60
  udp_stream      = 180
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `broadcast_ping` (Boolean) Whether the firewall responds to broadcast ping (hardening: leave `false`).
- `icmp` (Number) ICMP conntrack timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `other` (Number) Other-protocol conntrack timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `receive_redirects` (Boolean) Whether the firewall accepts ICMP redirects (hardening: leave `false`).
- `send_redirects` (Boolean) Whether the firewall sends ICMP redirects.
- `site_id` (String) Site ID the settings belong to. The settings are a singleton per site; use it as the import target. Changing this forces replacement.
- `syn_cookies` (Boolean) Whether SYN-flood cookie protection is enabled (hardening: leave `true`).
- `tcp_close` (Number) TCP CLOSE timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `tcp_close_wait` (Number) TCP CLOSE_WAIT timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `tcp_established` (Number) TCP ESTABLISHED timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `tcp_fin_wait` (Number) TCP FIN_WAIT timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `tcp_last_ack` (Number) TCP LAST_ACK timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `tcp_syn_receive` (Number) TCP SYN_RECEIVED timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `tcp_syn_sent` (Number) TCP SYN_SENT timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `tcp_time_wait` (Number) TCP TIME_WAIT timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `udp_other` (Number) UDP other timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
- `udp_stream` (Number) UDP stream timeout. Each is in seconds and must be within the range 1-2097151.
